stories.title: Remote discovery helper tools
stories.scope: Build bounded development/test helper tools for Device V2 onboarding discovery and later validation. The tools may query database device records, select real server/network devices, remotely log in, and collect evidence for Linux system-log files, server log-forwarding commands, network-device syslog commands, and SNMP trap commands.
stories.nonGoals: Do not turn these helpers into business feature paths. Business remote-server operations must go through controller-provided APIs. Do not change target-device configuration in this discovery story. Do not guess credentials, vendors, commands, log paths, or listener addresses. Do not write global scripts, modify production configuration, add dependencies, or hide remote-login failures.
acceptance: A helper-tool entrypoint exists under docs/openclaw-autodev/tools/d2on/ with clear command-line usage.
Documentation clearly states these helpers are only for development/testing and cannot be called by business code.
Development documents under docs/development/device-v2-onboarding-observability/ are updated if tool behavior or remote-operation boundaries change.
Evidence summaries are used to keep long-running task context small.
The tool reads real device candidates from the database or fails with an explicit connection/query error.
Read-only MySQL/API test access is used only when needed and credentials/tokens are not persisted in code, docs, config, or evidence.
The tool can run read-only remote inspection commands for selected server and network devices and records command, target, exit status, stdout/stderr summary, and timestamp.
Linux server discovery reports which configured system-log files exist from /var/log/syslog, /var/log/auth.log, /var/log/messages, and /var/log/secure.
Network-device discovery records observed vendor/platform clues and candidate syslog/SNMP trap command families without applying configuration.
The tool writes evidence under docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/discovery/ and exits non-zero on unknown credentials, unsupported devices, failed login, or ambiguous command templates.
Evidence summary is written to docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-000.md.
notes: The user explicitly authorized use of database servers and network devices for real remote-login discovery.
The user authorized read-only MySQL test access and X-Auth-Token backend API testing, but credentials/tokens must stay out of repository files.
Initial authorized test targets are server 192.168.100.7 and network device 172.32.2.14; see docs/openclaw-autodev/tools/d2on/test-targets.md.
Business feature implementation must use controller-provided APIs for all remote server operations.
Development documentation output location is docs/development/device-v2-onboarding-observability/.
Discovery is read-only. Configuration-changing commands belong to D2ON-004 or later explicit ensure/apply stories.

stories.title: Single-device onboarding evidence contract
stories.scope: Implement the minimal Device V2 onboarding evidence contract for one device. Store action results under device_v2_store_run.summary_json.onboarding.actions, add strict typed helpers for monitor/log onboarding actions, and add local config-file definitions for area syslog service and Linux system-log first_existing paths.
stories.nonGoals: Do not add a new orchestration state table, workflow engine, migrations, global API semantics, global styles, dependency upgrades, fallback defaults, or mock/pseudo success.
acceptance: Onboarding actions are typed and include category, target, method, status, timestamp, operator/action evidence, and explicit error detail when failed or unknown.
Retry planning skips success actions and selects only failed or unknown ensure actions.
Missing or invalid onboarding config fails loudly with a concrete error; no silent defaults are introduced.
Linux system-log strategy is represented as first_existing for /var/log/syslog, /var/log/auth.log, /var/log/messages, and /var/log/secure.
Relevant development documents under docs/development/device-v2-onboarding-observability/ are updated.
Evidence summary includes only durable decisions and command results needed by later stories.
Focused backend tests cover action merge, retry selection, and invalid config behavior.
Evidence summary is written to docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-001.md.
notes: Use the PRD as authority; do not infer syslog/SNMP trap area-service semantics beyond the explicit config contract.

stories.title: Device V2 onboarding plan and ensure API
stories.scope: Add single-device Device V2 backend APIs, following existing local Device V2 routing conventions, to read onboarding summary, build a plan, and execute explicit ensure actions for monitor/log onboarding. Server remote operations must call controller-provided APIs, while network-device operations may use the explicit network-device execution contract. Persist successful action evidence and expose failures without swallowing errors.
stories.nonGoals: Do not perform batch execution, change Device V1 behavior, change auth/tenant semantics, create global API helpers, or invent remote command results.
acceptance: API accepts exactly one Device V2 identifier and rejects missing or ambiguous identifiers.
Network monitoring success is represented by existing monitor-task dispatch success.
Network log onboarding first supports syslog ensure through explicit area-agent/syslog-listener evidence.
Server monitoring requires explicit user-selected agent or SNMP mode.
Server logging prefers agent when present; without agent, API returns a generated SSH configuration plan instead of executing it.
No business API path calls docs/openclaw-autodev/tools/d2on helper tools.
Backend API tests may use the authorized X-Auth-Token but must not persist it in repository files.
Errors from sync, monitor dispatch, config lookup, or remote execution are returned and persisted as failed evidence.
Relevant development documents under docs/development/device-v2-onboarding-observability/ are updated.
Backend runtime restart, if needed, is recorded in evidence.
Focused backend tests cover success, failed, and unknown retry behavior.
Evidence summary is written to docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-002.md.
notes: If an existing monitor-task dispatch contract cannot be called safely from Device V2 scope, stop as BLOCKED with code references instead of adding a fallback.

stories.scope: Complete the server-side log onboarding minimal path. When an agent is present, dispatch a log-forward task using the first_existing Linux system-log strategy. When no agent is present, generate and persist an SSH configuration plan without executing it.
stories.nonGoals: Do not implement broad OS discovery, unsupported log-file inference, batch execution, Prometheus/Loki arrival validation, or global logging strategy changes.
acceptance: Agent-present server log onboarding creates a real log-forward task request and records dispatch result.
Agent-absent server log onboarding returns an SSH configuration plan and records it as planned/skipped, not success.
Only configured first_existing system-log paths are used.
No missing path is treated as an error when the strategy is first_existing.

stories.title: Remove fake success from onboarding backend contract
stories.scope: Make the Device V2 onboarding backend internally honest. GET onboarding must return actions plus retryableActions and a derived summary from the latest real store run. PLAN must stop returning a fixed placeholder list and validate explicit server monitor mode only when required. ENSURE must stop using DefaultOnboardingConfig as the final runtime source, must stop writing hardcoded success evidence, and must merge onboarding results back into the latest real store run instead of inventing a synthetic success path.
stories.nonGoals: Do not add batch execution. Do not invent remote success when the real business contract is not safely callable. Do not widen scope into full SNMP trap support.
acceptance: GET onboarding returns actions, retryableActions, and a derived summary from the latest real store run.
PLAN onboarding is not a fixed placeholder response and rejects missing server monitor mode only when the target device actually requires it.
ENSURE onboarding no longer writes hardcoded success or dispatch_result=success placeholders.
ENSURE onboarding either loads a concrete config source or fails loudly with a concrete config-source error; it must not treat DefaultOnboardingConfig as a complete runtime source.
ENSURE onboarding merges action evidence into the latest real store run instead of creating a synthetic placeholder run.
notes: Start from docs/prd-autodev/device-v2-onboarding-observability/automation-repair-brief.md.
Current bug anchor: OneOPS/app/device/v2/api/device_v2_onboarding_api.go.

stories.title: Make EnsureOnboarding emit honest monitor/log action results
stories.scope: Refactor the backend single-device onboarding ensure path so it no longer fabricates a one-line syslog success. The ensure result must describe both monitor and log onboarding actions when applicable, respect explicit server monitor mode requirements, and record failed/unknown/blocked evidence when a real execution contract or template is missing instead of silently returning success.
nonGoals: Do not add batch execution.
Do not implement full SNMP trap delivery.
Do not guess remote success when the backend lacks a concrete contract.
acceptance: EnsureOnboarding no longer returns a hardcoded syslog success as the default happy path.
The response and stored onboarding summary can include both monitor and log actions when the device requires them.
Server monitor mode requirements remain explicit and affect the ensured action set instead of being ignored after planning.
When a concrete runtime contract is missing, the stored onboarding action records failed, unknown, or blocked evidence with an explicit reason instead of fabricated success.
Backend tests cover at least one honest non-success execution path and one server-monitor-mode-sensitive path.
notes: Current gap anchor: DeviceV2API.EnsureOnboarding still synthesizes a log success action and does not make the monitor side honest.
Use the existing onboarding action/evidence contract instead of inventing a new persistence model.

stories.title: Show non-success onboarding evidence clearly in DeviceV2ManagementRedesign
stories.scope: Harden the redesign execute flow so the actual start/ensure path does not feel like generic success when the backend reports failed, unknown, blocked, or retryable actions. The UI should surface backend action evidence, keep explicit monitor-mode requirements visible, and avoid unconditional success messaging after ensure returns non-success action states.
nonGoals: Do not redesign unrelated Device V2 panels.
Do not add batch execution UX.
Do not hide backend action details behind a generic success toast.
acceptance: The redesign execute flow does not show a generic success-only outcome when onboarding actions contain failed, unknown, or blocked items.
Retryable and non-success actions are surfaced from backend evidence after ensure returns.
Server monitor mode requirements remain explicit on the execute path and are not lost between plan and ensure.
The UI keeps the single-device-only rule and still renders backend action evidence directly.
notes: Current gap anchor: the execute path always raises a success toast once ensure returns, even though the backend evidence model already distinguishes non-success states.
